{{Short description|Men's football team representing French Polynesia}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Tahiti women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2025}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                     = Tahiti
| Nickname                 = Toa Aito (''Les guerriers de fer''; Iron Warriors)
| Badge                    = Tahiti FA (2016–).svg
| Badge_size               = 165px
| Association              = [[Tahitian Football Federation|Fédération Tahitienne de Football]] (FTF)
| Confederation            = [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (Oceania)
| Coach                    = [[Joël Fréchet]]
| Captain                  = [[Teaonui Tehau]]
| Most caps                = [[Teaonui Tehau]] (49)
| Top scorer               = [[Teaonui Tehau]] (31)
| Home Stadium             = [[Stade Pater Te Hono Nui]]
| FIFA Trigramme           = TAH
| FIFA Rank                = {{FIFA World Rankings|TAH}}
| FIFA max                 = 111
| FIFA max date            = August 2002
| FIFA min                 = 196
| FIFA min date            = April–May 2016
| Elo Rank                 = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Tahiti}}
| Elo max                  = 45
| Elo max date             = September 1983
| Elo min                  = 167
| Elo min date             = September 2011
| pattern_la1              = _macrontureis21rw
| pattern_b1               = _macrontureis21rw
| pattern_ra1              = _macrontureis21rw
| pattern_sh1              = 
| pattern_so1              = 
| leftarm1                 = FF0000
| body1                    = FF0000
| rightarm1                = FF0000
| shorts1                  = FF0000
| socks1                   = FF0000
| pattern_la2              = _macrontureis21wr
| pattern_b2               = _macrontureis21wr
| pattern_ra2              = _macrontureis21wr
| pattern_sh2              = 
| pattern_so2              = 
| leftarm2                 = FFFFFF
| body2                    = FFFFFF
| rightarm2                = FFFFFF
| shorts2                  = FFFFFF
| socks2                   = FFFFFF
| First game               = {{nowrap|{{fb|TAH|1946|name=French Polynesia}} 2–2 {{fb-rt|NZL}} <br /> ([[Papeete]], [[Tahiti]]; 21 September 1952)}}
| Largest win              = {{fb|TAH|1946}} 30–0 {{fb-rt|COK}} <br /> ([[Papeete]], [[Tahiti]]; 2 September 1971)
| Largest loss             = {{fb|NZL}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|TAH}} <br /> ([[Adelaide]], [[Australia]]; 4 June 2004) <br /> {{fb|ESP}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|TAH}} <br /> ([[Rio de Janeiro]], [[Brazil]]; 20 June 2013)
| Regional name            = [[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
| Regional cup apps        = 10
| Regional cup first       = [[1973 OFC Nations Cup|1973]]
| Regional cup best        = '''Champions''' ([[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]])
| Confederations cup apps  = 1
| Confederations cup first = [[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup|2013]]
| Confederations cup best  = Group stage ([[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup|2013]])
| 2ndRegional name         = [[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps     = 16
| 2ndRegional cup first    = [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]]
| 2ndRegional cup best     = '''Champions''' ([[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966]], [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]], [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]], [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983]], [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]])
| medaltemplates = {{MedalSport|Men's [[association football|football]]}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]}}
{{Medal|W|[[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012 Solomon Islands]]|}}
{{Medal|RU|[[1973 OFC Nations Cup|1973 New Zealand]]|}}
{{Medal|RU|[[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980 New Caledonia]]|}}
{{Medal|RU|[[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996 Oceania]]|}}
{{Medal|3rd|[[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002 New Zealand]]|}}
{{Medal|3rd|[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024 Fiji/Vanuatu]]|}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966 New Caledonia]]|}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975 Guam]]|}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979 Fiji]]|}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983 Western Samoa]]|}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995 Tahiti]]|}}
{{Medal|Silver|[[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969 Papua New Guinea]]|}}
{{Medal|Silver|[[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987 New Caledonia]]|}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963 Fiji]]|}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971 Tahiti]]|}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011 New Caledonia]]|}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[Football at the Pacific Mini Games|Pacific Mini Games]]}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1981 South Pacific Mini Games|1981 Solomon Islands]]|}}
{{Medal|Gold|[[Football at the 1993 South Pacific Mini Games|1993 Vanuatu]]|}}
{{MedalComp|[[Polynesia Cup]]}}
{{Medal|W|[[1994 Polynesia Cup|1994 Samoa]]|}}
{{Medal|W|[[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998 Cook Islands]]|}}
{{Medal|W|[[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000 Tahiti]]|}}
}}

The '''Tahiti national football team''' ({{langx|fr|Équipe de football de Tahiti}}; {{langx|ty|Te pŭpŭ tu'e popo a te fenua Tahiti}}) represents [[French Polynesia]]<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thefootballramble.com/blog/entry/trophies-in-paradise|title=Trophies in paradise|date=2 October 2012|access-date=18 June 2013|work=The Football Ramble|archive-date=5 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305080839/http://www.thefootballramble.com/blog/entry/trophies-in-paradise|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.scotsman.com/scotland-on-sunday/sport/football/tahiti-ready-for-confederations-cup-1-2960604|title=Tahiti ready for Confederations Cup|date=9 June 2013|access-date=18 June 2013|website=The Scotsman|archive-date=3 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200203173006/https://www.scotsman.com/sport/football/tahiti-ready-for-confederations-cup-1-2960604|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://footballrepublik.com/tahiti-in-the-land-of-the-giants/|title=IN THE LAND OF GIANTS&nbsp;– Tahiti set for Confederations Cup adventure|date=14 June 2013|access-date=5 July 2013|website=Football Republik|archive-date=24 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211124063412/http://footballrepublik.com/tahiti-in-the-land-of-the-giants/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.roadtobrazil.com/teams/ofc/tahiti/|title=Tahiti national football team|access-date=5 July 2013|website=Road To Brazil|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130705143643/http://www.roadtobrazil.com/teams/ofc/tahiti/|archive-date=5 July 2013|df=dmy-all}}</ref> in men's international [[Association football|football]], and is controlled by the [[Tahitian Football Federation]]. The team consists of a selection of players from French Polynesia, including [[Tahiti]]; they have competed in the [[Oceania Football Confederation]] (OFC) since 1990.

Tahiti is traditionally one of the stronger footballing nations of the [[Pacific Islands]], with the second-best record at the [[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]], winning five gold medals. They were runners-up in the first three editions of the [[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]] ([[1973 Oceania Cup|1973]], [[1980 Oceania Cup|1980]], [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]). Tahiti eventually won the competition in [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]], becoming the first team other than [[Australia men's national soccer team|Australia]] and [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]] to win the title. The feat qualified Tahiti to the [[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup]] held in [[Brazil]]. It qualified for the [[2009 FIFA U-20 World Cup]] in Egypt, becoming the first archipelago from Oceania to compete in a FIFA-sanctioned tournament, a feat it repeated in [[2019 FIFA U-20 World Cup|2019]] in Poland.

==History==
Tahiti played its first full match on 21 September 1952, at home against [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]], drawing 2–2. Seven days later, the two teams played again and New Zealand won 5–3. On 30 September, they played each other for a third time, and Tahiti gained its first victory, by 2–0 with a spectacular brace from Dutch van der Linde. However, it is unknown whether Arthur Morgan approved of such.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablest/tahiti-intres.html |title=Tahiti International Matches |website=Rsssf.com |access-date=23 June 2013 |archive-date=9 August 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220809232933/https://www.rsssf.org/tablest/tahiti-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

In September 1953, Tahiti played three matches in [[New Caledonia]] against [[New Caledonia national football team|its national side]], losing the first 5–0 and the later two 4–1. They then travelled to the New Hebrides (now [[Vanuatu]]) and beat [[Vanuatu national football team|its national side]] 4–2 twice. In 1969, touring World Champions [[England]] beat Tahiti 4–1 in an exhibition match.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.englandfootballonline.com/matchrsl/MatchRslUnoffpg2.html |title=England's matches: unofficial 1946 onwards |publisher=England Football Online |date= |access-date=6 July 2021 |archive-date=27 March 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090639/http://www.englandfootballonline.com/matchrsl/MatchRslUnoffpg2.html |url-status=live }}</ref> In 1989, under the leadership of Napoleon Spitz, the official federation was created.

Tahiti entered its first World Cup qualification with the aim of reaching the [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994 World Cup]], held in the United States. They were placed in [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)#Group A|Group A]] alongside [[Australia men's national soccer team|Australia]] and the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]], and played their first match away to the Solomon Islands in [[Honiara]] on 11 July 1992. Eric Etaeta equalised for Tahiti to make it 1–1 in the 76th minute. On 11 September, Tahiti hosted Australia in [[Papeete]] and lost 3–0. The next fixture was again against Australia, and resulted in a 2–0 away defeat in [[Brisbane]] on 20 September. On 9 October, in Papeete, Tahiti defeated the Solomon Islands 4–2. Tahiti's first goal was scored as an 8th-minute penalty from Reynald Temarii, a politician and current president of the OFC. However, Tahiti finished second to Australia in the group and did not advance.

===2012 OFC Nations Cup===
In [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]], the new edition of the tournament occurred in the Solomon Islands with the host country, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Vanuatu, Tahiti, [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]], [[Papua New Guinea national football team|Papua New Guinea]] and [[Samoa national football team|Samoa]] (winner of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification&nbsp;– OFC first round|qualifying tournament]]) playing the competition. Tahiti defeated New Caledonia in [[2012 OFC Nations Cup#Final|the final]] in [[Lawson Tama Stadium]] 1–0 with a goal by [[Steevy Chong Hue]] and became the first team other than Australia (no longer part of OFC) and New Zealand to be crowned Oceania champions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/preliminaries/news/newsid=1646980/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120615194144/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/preliminaries/news/newsid=1646980/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=15 June 2012|title=Glorious Tahiti claim maiden Oceania crown|date=10 June 2012|website=FIFA.com|publisher=FIFA|access-date=12 June 2012 }}</ref>

===2013 Confederations Cup===
[[File:Spain-Tahiti, Confederations Cup 2013 (05).jpg|thumb|336x336px|Tahiti players playing against [[2010 FIFA World Cup]] champions [[Spain national football team|Spain]]]]
By winning the 2012 OFC Nations Cup, Tahiti qualified for the [[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup|2013 Confederations Cup]], held in Brazil, for the first time.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.fifa.com/confederationscup/news/newsid=2030236/index.html|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20130317234034/http://www.fifa.com/confederationscup/news/newsid=2030236/index.html|url-status= dead|archive-date= 17 March 2013|title=Proud Tahiti No1 aims to enjoy Brazil challenge
|date=14 March 2013|website=FIFA.com|publisher=FIFA|access-date=15 May 2013 }}</ref> On 17 June 2013, Tahiti lost 1–6 to [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] in the 2013 Confederations Cup in [[Belo Horizonte]], with [[Jonathan Tehau]] scoring the goal for Tahiti in the second half with a header from a corner; Tahiti fans still rejoiced in the prospect of scoring a goal in an international tournament.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/22896351|title=Nigeria 6−1 Tahiti|date=17 June 2013|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=24 June 2013|archive-date=23 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130623055709/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/22896351|url-status=live}}</ref> On 20 June, Tahiti lost 10–0 against [[Spain national football team|Spain]] to equal their largest ever lost against [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]] nine years earlier.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/22896367|title=Spain 10−0 Tahiti|date=20 June 2013|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=24 June 2013|archive-date=23 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130623055640/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/22896367|url-status=live}}</ref> On 23 June 2013, Tahiti was beaten 8–0 by [[Uruguay national football team|Uruguay]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/22924993|title=Uruguay 8−0 Tahiti|date=23 June 2013|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=24 June 2013|archive-date=23 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130623054822/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/22924993|url-status=live}}</ref>

In all, Tahiti conceded 24 goals and scored 1 to end with a goal differential of −23, the worst of any national team in any major competition.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/confederationscup/archive/brazil2013/statistics/teams/goal-scored.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150922162156/http://www.fifa.com/confederationscup/archive/brazil2013/statistics/teams/goal-scored.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=22 September 2015|title=FIFA Confederations Cup Brazil 2013 - Statistics - Teams - Top goals|date=1 July 2013|website=FIFA.com|publisher=FIFA|access-date=28 January 2018 }}</ref> However, even with the poor record and heavy defeats, Tahiti's [[underdog (term)|underdog]] qualities gathered significant respect from the people of Brazil, who always cheered for them in every match.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://copadomundo.uol.com.br/noticias/redacao/2013/06/23/tecnico-confirma-carisma-do-taiti-e-cumprimenta-jornalistas-na-despedida.htm|title=Técnico confirma 'carisma' do Taiti e cumprimenta jornalistas na despedida|date=23 June 2013|work=[[universo Online|UOL Esporte]]|language=pt|access-date=24 June 2013|archive-date=25 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130625023536/http://copadomundo.uol.com.br/noticias/redacao/2013/06/23/tecnico-confirma-carisma-do-taiti-e-cumprimenta-jornalistas-na-despedida.htm|url-status=live}}</ref> Spanish coach [[Vicente del Bosque]], and strikers [[Fernando Torres]] and [[David Villa]]&nbsp;– who scored four and three goals respectively against Tahiti&nbsp;– complimented the team's fair play.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://globoesporte.globo.com/futebol/copa-das-confederacoes/noticia/2013/06/nada-de-pancadaria-fair-play-do-taiti-em-goleada-arranca-elogios-da-furia.html|title=Nada de pancadaria: 'fair play' do Taiti em goleada arranca elogios da Fúria|date=21 June 2013|work=[[Rede Globo|Globo Esporte]]|language=pt|access-date=24 June 2013|archive-date=24 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130624074919/http://globoesporte.globo.com/futebol/copa-das-confederacoes/noticia/2013/06/nada-de-pancadaria-fair-play-do-taiti-em-goleada-arranca-elogios-da-furia.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Rivalries==
A historical sporting rivalry exists between the two French Pacific overseas collectivities, Tahiti and [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]]. They compete regularly in regional and, since 2006, international competitions. In 2012, Tahiti led the number of titles won (1 [[OFC Nations Cup]], 5 gold medals at the [[South Pacific Games]], 2 at the South Pacific mini-games, against 6 gold at the South Pacific Games for New Caledonia). As of their last match in 2018, out of the 62 matches played since 1953, the New Caledonia has 28 wins against 25 for Tahiti and 9 draws.{{citation needed|date=January 2025}}

== Team image ==
{{commons|Tahiti national football team kits}}
===Kit sponsorship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
|-
! Kit supplier
! Period
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Adidas]] ||2000–2003
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] ||2004–2012
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|United States}} [[Nike, Inc|Nike]] || 2013–2021
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]] || 2022–2023
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Puma SE|Puma]] || 2024–present
|-
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{See also|Tahiti national football team results (2000–present)}}

==Coaching history==
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|Tahiti|1946}} [[Freddy Vernaudon]] (1973)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} {{flagicon|Vietnam}} [[Richard Van Sam]] (1980)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[François Ferez]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Bernard Vahirua]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|Italy}} {{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Umberto Mottini]] (1995–1996)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Gerard Kautai]] (1996)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} {{flagicon|Vietnam}} [[Richard Van Sam]] (1997)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Alain Rousseau]] (1998)
*{{flagicon|Australia}} [[Leon Gardikiotis]] (1999–2000)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} {{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Patrick Jacquemet]] (2001–2003)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Gerard Kautai]] (2004–2007)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Eddy Etaeta]] (2010–2015)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Ludovic Graugnard]] (2015–2018)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Naea Bennett]] (2018–2019)
*{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Samuel Garcia (footballer, born 1975)|Samuel Garcia]] (2019–2025)
*{{flagicon|France}} [[Joël Fréchet]] (2026–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] game against [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]] on 21 March 2025.<ref>[https://x.com/windowintlpod/status/1901585868277661955/photo/1 La liste officielle]</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 21 March 2025, after the match against [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Tevaearai Tamatai]]|age={{bda|2001|1|15|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Vénus|Vénus]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=GK|name=[[Teave Teamotuaitau]]|age={{bda|1992|4|17|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[François Decoret]]|age={{bda|1999|3|10|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Pirae|Pirae]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Taumihau Tiatia]]|age={{bda|1991|7|25|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Pirae|Pirae]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Matatia Paama]]|age={{bda|df=y|1992|10|3}}|caps=22|goals=1|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Pothin Poma]]|age={{bda|1997|2|13|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=DF|name=[[Kévin Barbe]]|age={{bda|df=y|1997|9|2}}|caps=15|goals=1|club=[[Tahiti United FC|Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[Mauri Heitaa]]|age={{bda|1999|7|31|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=DF|name=[[François Hapipi]]|age={{bda|1999|3|10|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Fencibles United]]|clubnat=NZL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=DF|name=[[Téva Lossec]]|age={{bda|2002|12|3|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[Campbell Fighting Camels]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=DF|name=[[Matéo Degrumelle]]|age={{bda|2003|8|22|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=3|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=MF|name=[[Vahia Tetuaroa]]|age={{bda|df=y|2000|1|5}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Vénus|Vénus]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Roonui Tehau]]|age={{bda|1999|12|15|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=MF|name=[[Frank Papaura]]|age={{bda|2005|4|6|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Mana Teniau]]|age={{bda|df=y|2005|1|15}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=MF|name=[[Tauhiti Keck]]|age={{bda|1994|8|1|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=6|club=[[A.S. Vénus|Vénus]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=MF|name=[[Manoa Flores]]|age={{bda|df=y|2005|11|9}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Vénus|Vénus]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Victor Snow]]|age={{bda|1991|10|27|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[A.S. Tefana|Tefana]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=MF|name=[[Manuarii Shan]]|age={{bda|2004|2|23|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Benoît Mathon]]|age={{bda|1989|4|5|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=2|club=[[L'Union Saint-Jean FC|Union Saint-Jean]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Teaonui Tehau]]|age={{bda|1992|9|1|df=y}}|caps=49|goals=31|club=[[Tahiti United]]|clubnat=TAH}}
{{nat fs g end}}

*[[A.S. Vénus|Vénus]] forward [[Eddy Kaspard]] was also called up. He died on 14 August 2025, aged 24.

==Player records==
{{Updated|21 March 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Tahiti |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/182/Tahiti.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Tahiti.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Teaonui Tehau.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Teaonui Tehau is Tahiti's top goalscorer and their most-capped player.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|'''[[Teaonui Tehau]]'''
|49
|31
|2011–present
|-
|2
|align=left|[[Angelo Tchen]]
|34
|1
|2001–2018
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|align=left|[[Heimano Bourebare]]
|30
|1
|2010–2023
|-
|align=left|'''[[Alvin Tehau]]'''
|30
|9
|2010–present
|-
|rowspan="2"|5
|align=left|[[Steevy Chong Hue]]
|28
|8
|2010–2016
|-
|align=left|[[Xavier Samin]]
|28
|0
|2001–2013
|-
|7
|align=left|[[Jonathan Tehau]]
|26
|5
|2011–2017
|-
|8
|align=left|[[Vincent Simon (footballer)|Vincent Simon]]
|25
|1
|2004–2016
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|align=left|[[Stanley Atani]]
|24
|6
|2010–2019
|-
|align=left|[[Tetahio Auraa]]
|24
|1
|1996–2008
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=175px|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Teaonui Tehau]]'''
|31
|49
|{{#expr: 31/49 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Felix Tagawa]]
|14
|23
|{{#expr: 14/23 round 2}}
|2000–2004
|-
|3
|align="left"|[[Naea Bennett]]
|12
|16
|{{#expr: 12/16 round 2}}
|1996–2010
|-
|4
|align="left"|'''[[Alvin Tehau]]'''
|9
|30
|{{#expr: 9/30 round 2}}
|2010–present
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[Steevy Chong Hue]]
|8
|28
|{{#expr: 8/28 round 2}}
|2010–2016
|-
|rowspan="2"|6
|align="left"|[[Jean-Loup Rousseau]]
|7
|15
|{{#expr: 7/15 round 2}}
|1992–2000
|-
|align="left"|[[Lorenzo Tehau]]
|7
|20
|{{#expr: 7/20 round 2}}
|2010–2017
|-
|rowspan="2"|8
|align="left"|'''[[Tauhiti Keck]]'''
|6
|19
|{{#expr: 6/19 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|align="left"|[[Stanley Atani]]
|6
|24
|{{#expr: 6/24 round 2}}
|2010–2019
|-
|rowspan="4"|10
|align="left"|'''[[Roonui Tinirauarii]]'''
|5
|10
|{{#expr: 5/10 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|align="left"|[[Tamatoa Tetauira]]
|5
|11
|{{#expr: 5/11 round 2}}
|2016–2019
|-
|align="left"|[[Hiro Poroiae]]
|5
|12
|{{#expr: 5/12 round 2}}
|2007–2013
|-
|align="left"|[[Jonathan Tehau]]
|5
|26
|{{#expr: 5/26 round 2}}
|2011–2017
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=16|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Host
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|colspan=2|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]
|colspan=8|''No national representative''
|colspan=6|''No national representative''
|-
|colspan=2|[[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]] to [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]||align=left|{{flag|United States}}
|rowspan=9 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|1
|2
|5
|8
|-
|[[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]||align=left|{{flag|France|1974}}
|4
|0
|1
|3
|2
|12
|-
|[[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]||align=left|{{flag|South Korea|1997}}<br>{{flag|Japan}}
|4
|3
|0
|1
|14
|6
|-
|[[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]||align=left|{{flag|Germany}}
|9
|3
|3
|3
|7
|25
|-
|[[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]||align=left|{{flag|South Africa}}
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|6
|-
|[[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]||align=left|{{flag|Brazil}}
|11
|6
|0
|5
|22
|17
|-
|[[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]||align=left|{{flag|Russia}}
|7
|3
|2
|2
|14
|7
|-
|[[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]||align=left|{{flag|Qatar}}
|2||0||0||2||1||4
|-
|[[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]||align=left|{{flag|Canada}}<br>{{flag|Mexico}}<br>{{flag|United States}}
|4||2||0||2||5||6
|-
|[[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]||align=left|{{flag|Morocco}}<br>{{flag|Portugal}}<br>{{flag|Spain}}
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|[[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]||align=left|{{flag|Saudi Arabia}}
|-
!colspan=2|Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/9|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!49
!19
!8
!22
!72
!91
|}

===OFC Nations Cup===
{{main|Tahiti at the OFC Nations Cup}}
{|class="wikitable nowrap" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|Oceania Cup / OFC Nations Cup]] record
!rowspan=14|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|NZL}} [[1973 Oceania Cup|1973]]
|'''[[1973 Oceania Cup final|Runners-up]]'''||'''2nd'''||'''5'''||'''2'''||'''2'''||'''1'''||'''7'''||'''4'''||'''[[1973 Oceania Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''No qualifiers were held''
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[1980 Oceania Cup|1980]]
|'''[[1980 Oceania Cup final|Runners-up]]'''||'''2nd'''||'''4'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''1'''||'''23'''||'''9'''||'''Squad'''
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]
|'''[[1996 OFC Nations Cup final|Runners-up]]'''||'''2nd'''||'''4'''||'''2'''||'''0'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''12'''||'''[[1996 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|3
|3
|0
|0
|10
|1
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|AUS}} [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]]
|'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''4'''||'''1'''||'''0'''||'''3'''||'''8'''||'''10'''||'''[[1998 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|4
|4
|0
|0
|27
|1
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]]
|Group stage||5th||2||0||0||2||2||5||[[2000 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]
|4||4||0||0||30||2
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|NZL}} [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002]]
|'''Third place'''||'''3rd'''||'''5'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''2'''||'''8'''||'''9'''||'''[[2002 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|colspan=6|''Directly qualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|AUS}} [[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]]
|Group stage||5th||5||1||1||3||2||24||[[2004 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]
|4
|2
|2
|0
|5
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[2008 OFC Nations Cup|2008]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|6
|-
|style="background:gold;"|{{flagicon|SOL}} [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]]
|style="background:gold;"|'''[[2012 OFC Nations Cup final|Champions]]'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''1st'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''5'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''5'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''0'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''0'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''20'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''5'''
|style="background:gold;"|'''[[2012 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|colspan=6 rowspan=3|''Directly qualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]]
|Group stage||5th||3||1||2||0||7||3||[[2016 OFC Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} {{flagicon|VAN}} [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]
|'''Third place'''||'''3rd'''||'''5'''||'''2'''||'''1'''||'''2'''||'''5'''||'''8'''||'''[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]'''
|-
!Total||1 Title||10/11||42||20||6||16||85||89||—
!19
!14
!3
!2
!74
!11
|}

===FIFA Confederations Cup===
{{main|Tahiti at the OFC Nations Cup}}

{|class="wikitable"  style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[FIFA Confederations Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
|-
|[[1992 King Fahd Cup|1992]] to [[1995 King Fahd Cup|1995]]
|colspan=9|''No OFC representative invited''
|-
|[[1997 FIFA Confederations Cup|1997]] to [[2009 FIFA Confederations Cup|2009]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup|2013]]
|Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||1||24||[[2013 FIFA Confederations Cup squads#Tahiti|Squad]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RUS}} [[2017 FIFA Confederations Cup|2017]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|-
!Total||Group stage||1/10||3||0||0||3||1||24||—
|}

===Polynesia Cup===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[Polynesia Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- bgcolor=gold
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[1994 Polynesia Cup|1994]]
|'''Champions'''
|'''1st'''
|'''3'''
|'''3'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''10'''
|'''1'''
|- bgcolor=gold
|{{flagicon|COK}} [[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]]
|'''Champions'''
|'''1st'''
|'''4'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''27'''
|'''1'''
|- bgcolor=gold
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000]]
|'''Champions'''
|'''1st'''
|'''4'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''30'''
|'''2'''
|-
!Total
!3 Titles
!3/3
!11
!11
!0
!0
!67
!4
|}

===Pacific Games===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ|colonial}} [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]]
|'''Bronze medal'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''2'''
|'''1'''
|'''0'''
|'''1'''
|'''19'''
|'''2'''
|- style="background:gold;"
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966]]
|'''Gold medal'''
|'''1st'''
|'''4'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''14'''
|'''3'''
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|PNG|1965}} [[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969]]
|'''Silver medal'''
|'''2nd'''
|'''5'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''19'''
|'''7'''
|- style="background:#c96;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|TAH|1946}} [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
|'''Bronze medal'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''4'''
|'''2'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''41'''
|'''5'''
|- style="background:gold;"
|{{flagicon|GUM}} [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]]
|'''Gold medal'''
|'''1st'''
|'''5'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''1'''
|'''12'''
|'''6'''
|- style="background:gold;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]]
|'''Gold medal'''
|'''1st'''
|'''5'''
|'''5'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''33'''
|'''2'''
|- style="background:gold;"
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983]]
|'''Gold medal'''
|'''1st'''
|'''5'''
|'''5'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''25'''
|'''2'''
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987]]
|'''Silver medal'''
|'''2nd'''
|'''5'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''9'''
|'''4'''
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 1991 South Pacific Games|1991]]
|Group stage
|6th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|15
|5
|- style="background:gold;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]]
|'''Gold medal'''
|'''1st'''
|'''6'''
|'''6'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''35'''
|'''2'''
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 2003 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2003]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''6'''
|'''3'''
|'''0'''
|'''3'''
|'''25'''
|'''7'''
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2007]]
|Group stage
|6th
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|6
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|NCL|football}} [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011]]
|'''Bronze medal'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''6'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''2'''
|'''28'''
|'''9'''
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 2015 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2015]]
|'''Silver medal'''
|'''2nd'''
|'''5'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''34'''
|'''4'''
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2019 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|Group stage
|5th
|5
|3
|0
|2
|19
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|SOL}} [[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
|Group stage
|5th
|4
|3
|1
|0
|9
|1
|-
!Total
!5 Gold medals
!16/16
!74
!50
!7
!17
!339
!71
|}

== Head-to-head record ==
{{Main|Tahiti national football team records and statistics}}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (3): [[1973 Oceania Cup|1973]], [[1980 Oceania Cup|1980]], [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (2): [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002]], [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Gold medals (5)''': [[1966 Pacific Games |1966]], [[1975 South Pacific Games |1975]], [[1979 South Pacific Games |1979]], [[1983 South Pacific Games |1983]], [[1995 South Pacific Games |1995]]
** {{Silver2}} Silver medals (2): [[1969 South Pacific Games|1969]], [[1987 South Pacific Games|1987]]
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medals (3): [[1963 South Pacific Games|1963]], [[1971 South Pacific Games|1971]], [[2011 Pacific Games|2011]]
* '''[[Polynesia Cup]]'''
**  {{Gold1}} '''Champions (3)''': [[1994 Polynesia Cup|1994]], [[1998 Polynesia Cup|1998]], [[2000 Polynesia Cup|2000]]

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
|1||3||2||6
|-
!Total!!1!!3!!2!!6
|}

==Notes==
{{Reflist|group=note}}

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* [https://www.ftf.pf/ Official website]
* [https://www.oceaniafootball.com/tahiti/ Tahiti] at [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/TAH Tahiti] at [[FIFA]]

{{Tahiti national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Tahiti squads
|bg=#CE1126
|fg=white
|bordercolor=gainsboro
|list1=
{{Tahiti squad 1973 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 1998 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2002 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2004 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2012 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2013 FIFA Confederations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2016 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Tahiti squad 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup}}
}}
{{Tahiti national football team managers}}
{{Football in Tahiti}}
{{Football in France}}
{{2013 FIFA Confederations Cup finalists}}
{{OFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of French Polynesia}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Tahiti Men's National Football Team}}
[[Category:Tahiti national football team| ]]
[[Category:Tahiti men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:National football teams of Overseas France]]
[[Category:Oceanian national association football teams]]
